steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Text 1 establishes that Gondwanan species originated ~80 million years ago when the island split. Text 2 gives crown ages for Grande Terre's current clades: stick insects at 41.1 Ma, nearly everything else more recent. The question asks what logically follows from combining these two texts.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: misapplies the Goodeniaceae date to Gondwanan origins — unsupported by either text. ✗
  • Choice B: reverses the logical relationship between clade age and species origin. ✗
  • Choice C — Gondwanan species cannot be members of Grande Terre's current clades: a species from 80 Ma cannot belong to a clade whose crown age is only 41.1 Ma or younger — the clade didn't exist yet. ✓
  • Choice D: makes a specific unsupported claim not derivable from the crown-age data. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
